producer_note: Pomerol estate dating to the 18th century, Moueix's first vineyard purchase in 1950. 18.7-ha vineyard on the Pomerol plateau planted to 91% Merlot, 6% Cabernet Franc, 3% Petit Verdot.
Pomerol estate owned by the Barreau-Badar family since 1925. 5.5-ha single-block vineyard on the central plateau planted 70% Merlot, 25% Cabernet Franc, 5% Cabernet Sauvignon.
Maremma estate founded 1985 by Elisabetta Geppetti, 'Lady of Morellino'. 420 ha including 85 ha vineyards in Morellino di Scansano DOCG; flagship Saffredi Toscana IGT.
Rutherford AVA estate established by Hi Sang Lee in 2005 on an 1883 winery site. Boutique production under 1,000 cases/year, winemaker Chris Cooney with consultant Philippe Melka.
Pomerol-inspired Merlot project founded 2002 by winemaker Heidi Barrett and John Schwartz. Sourced from East Rutherford hills vineyards in Napa Valley; ~91% Merlot, 9% Cabernet Franc.
19-generation family estate at Monfalletto in La Morra, commercial winery since 1940s. 28 ha of Nebbiolo, Dolcetto, Barbera, Arneis; organic since 2013; Barolo Enrico VI from Villero.

Founded 1872 by William Ponsot in Morey-Saint-Denis; largest landowner in Clos de la Roche Grand Cru (>3 ha). Famous Clos des Monts Luisants 1er Cru is the only premier cru Burgundy made entirely from Aligote.
Founded 1992 by former Disney Studios president Rich Frank at the historic 1884 Larkmead Winery in Calistoga. 380+ acres across multiple Napa Valley appellations; Cabernet, Chardonnay, sparkling.
Founded 1961 by Giacomo Bologna in Rocchetta Tanaro, Asti. Pioneer of single-vineyard, barrique-aged Barbera with Bricco dell'Uccellone (1982). Now run by daughter Raffaella and son Beppe.
Cru Classe in Leognan, owned by the Wilmers family since 1998. 28 ha planted 65% Cabernet Sauvignon, 25% Merlot, 10% Cabernet Franc; ~4 ha of vines >120 years old.
Montalcino estate founded 1994, purchased 2007 by Marilisa Allegrini and now run by daughters Carlotta and Caterina. CasaClima-certified underground winery; 100% estate-grown Sangiovese.
Iconic traditionalist Barolo producer, founded officially 1918 by Giulio Mascarello. ~5 ha; lone Barolo blends Cannubi, San Lorenzo, Rue, Rocche; today led by Maria Teresa Mascarello.
Margaux Second Growth (1855) owned by the Quie family since 1946. 28.5 ha planted 65% Cabernet Sauvignon, 25% Merlot, 5% Cabernet Franc, 5% Petit Verdot; modernized 2013.
Historic Barolo house, estate of Marchesi Falletti acquired by Pietro Abbona in 1929; 5th-generation Abbona family. ~200 ha across Langhe/Roero/Monferrato including Cannubi cru.
Verona estate founded 1857 by Giovan Battista and Gaetano Bertani; Amarone pioneer in 1950s. 200+ ha across Valpolicella Classica, Valpantena, Soave and Lake Garda.
Founded 1978 by Luciano Sandrone (d. 2023) on Cannubi Boschis cru; 27 ha across Piedmont. Iconic Barolo Aleste (formerly Cannubi Boschis) and Barolo Le Vigne; modern-traditional style.

Winery in Russian River Valley near Santa Rosa on an 1881 historic site. Sources from Russian River Valley, Napa Valley, Santa Cruz Mountains; produces under Martin Ray, Angeline and Synthesis labels.
Tiny ~1.8-ha Pomerol estate on the plateau between Trotanoy and Le Pin, owned by Vignobles Pere-Verge since 2006. 100% Merlot; <400 cases per year aged in 100% new French oak.
Domaine started 2017 by Simon Colin (son of Philippe Colin) in Chassagne-Montrachet, receiving family vineyards from 2021. Premiers Crus Les Vergers, Morgeot, Les Chaumees plus Puligny La Garenne.
Branded-wine arm of Baron Philippe de Rothschild SA; Mouton Cadet (created 1930) is the world's biggest-selling Bordeaux brand, blended from selected wines across Bordeaux's appellations.
18th-century Chassagne-Montrachet estate, family-owned since 1939; Jean-Pierre Cournut owner-winemaker since 1993. ~13 ha in Chassagne plus 2 ha in Santenay; 70% Premier/Grand Cru.

Maconnais negociant launched 2001 by Jean-Philippe and Jean-Guillaume Bret alongside their estate Domaine de la Soufrandiere in Vinzelles. Micro-cuvees, organic/biodynamic, focus on Pouilly-Vinzelles.
Oakville winery founded 1885 by John Benson, restored 1979 by Gil and Beth Nickel. Estate-bottled Cabernet Sauvignon and Chardonnay; on the National Register of Historic Places.
Founded 1986 by Jayson Pahlmeyer; 'California Mouton' aspiration. Sources from mountain vineyards across Napa Valley and Sonoma Coast incl. Waters Ranch (Atlas Peak) and Wayfarer (Sonoma Coast).
Cult Napa Cabernet producer with winemaker Celia Welch. Sources from small premium vineyards across mid-to-southern Napa Valley appellations rather than a single sub-AVA.
Margaux Third Growth (1855) under Emmanuel Cruse; Jacky Lorenzetti acquired 50% in 2013. 41 ha planted 62% Cabernet Sauvignon, 38% Merlot, adjacent to Chateaux Palmer and Margaux.
Estate founded 2000 by Lou and Bobbie Kapcsandy on a 20-acre State Lane Vineyard in Yountville (former Beringer Private Reserve site); inaugural vintage 2005.
Brunello estate founded 1984 by artist Sandro Chia in northwestern Montalcino, now run with son Filippo Chia. 16 ha at 450 m elevation; labels feature Chia's Transavanguardia art.

Family winery founded 2006 by Luc and Jodie Morlet, based in St. Helena since 2010. Vineyards across Napa Valley and Sonoma Coast (Knights Valley Mon Chevalier, Coombsville, Sonoma Coast Pinot/Chardonnay).
4.5-ha Pomerol jewel created when Christian Moueix took over former Chateau Certan-Giraud in 1999. Planted 70% Merlot, 30% Cabernet Franc; ~18,000 bottles annually.
Maconnais negociant founded 1990 by Jean-Marie Guffens (Belgium) and Maine Heynen, focused on Chardonnay; works exclusively with Maconnais fruit today.
Oakville winery founded 1966 by Robert Mondavi at the To Kalon Vineyard, the first major post-Prohibition Napa winery. To Kalon CCOF organic-certified.
Founded 1961 by Dino Carletti; 170 ha of vineyards across Montepulciano, Montefollonico, Maremma and Cortona. Best known for Vino Nobile di Montepulciano from Sangiovese (Prugnolo Gentile).
Valpolicella Classica producer since 1772, owned by the Boscaini family. Leading Amarone house with 5 Amarone brands (incl. Costasera); ~12 million bottles/year.
Founded 2002, winery and estate vineyards in Stags Leap District; sources from premium Napa vineyards (Beckstoffer Dr. Crane, To Kalon, Farella). Flagship 'The Bard' Cab blend.
Calistoga winery famed for the 1973 Chardonnay that won the 1976 Judgment of Paris. Founded 1882, refounded 1972 by Jim Barrett; Bo Barrett CEO, winemaker Matt Crafton.
Grower in Cramant since the late 19th century; Aurelien Suenen took over 2009. 3 ha of Chardonnay across grand crus Cramant, Chouilly and Oiry; certified organic 2019.
Alto Piemonte estate, family winemaking since 1920, eponymous winery est. 1958 by Giancarlo Travaglini. 59 ha of Nebbiolo on volcanic soils; #1 selling Gattinara worldwide.
Provence estate near La Motte acquired 2006 by Sacha Lichine; producer of Whispering Angel, the global benchmark Provence rose sold in 100+ countries. Grenache-led blends.
Beaune-based small negociant established 2007 by Benjamin Leroux (former manager of Domaine du Comte Armand). 8 ha across ~50 appellations spanning Cote de Nuits and Cote de Beaune.
Founded 1981 by Bruno and Brigitte Widmer; three estates across Castellina and Radda in Chianti Classico plus Maremma. 80 ha total; flagship Il Blu (Sangiovese/Merlot/Cabernet).
Margaret River's founding winery, established 1967 by Dr Tom Cullity; Holmes a Court family-owned since 1987. Three vineyards in the region, focus on Cabernet Sauvignon and Chardonnay.
Family winery founded 1993 by Claus Janzen (ex-Caymus) on slopes overlooking St. Helena. Cabernet-driven blends with Sangiovese and other varieties; sources across Napa Valley.
Mazzei family estate since 1435 in Castellina in Chianti, with vineyards also in Castelnuovo Berardenga and Radda in Chianti. 110 ha of vines on 650 ha; led by 25th-generation Mazzei family.
Margaux Third Growth (1855), owned by the Zuger family since 1955. 30 ha planted 50% Cabernet Sauvignon, 35% Merlot, 10% Cabernet Franc, 5% Petit Verdot; consultant Michel Rolland since 1995.
Barbaresco family estate, winemaking since 2005 from La Berchialla farmhouse; ~7 ha including Roncagliette and Starderi crus, plus Altavilla in Langhe. Organic since 2018.
Serralunga d'Alba estate founded 1896 by Giovanni Massolino; fourth-generation brothers Franco and Roberto. 23 ha mostly in Serralunga, crus Margheria, Parafada, Vigna Rionda.
Sixth-generation family winery in La Morra founded 1850s. 16 ha in Langhe and Roero; iconic crus Brunate and La Serra in La Morra. Traditional long macerations and aging in large botti.

Cabernet specialist founded 1972 by Ray Duncan and Justin Meyer. Two wineries: Oakville (Napa Valley) and Healdsburg (Alexander Valley); produces separate Napa and Alexander Valley Cabernets aged in American oak.

Montecucco DOC flagship in Cinigiano (Monte Amiata foothills) founded 1998 by Maria Iris Bertarelli and Claudio Tipa; first vintage 2000. 1,200 ha (10% under vine), organic; Gambero Rosso 'Cellar of the Year' 2014.
Captûre Wines (Jackson Family Wines) founded 2008 by Sam Teakle. Sources from high-elevation vineyards in Pine Mountain-Cloverdale Peak, Alexander Valley, Sonoma County and Lake County.
Founded 1890 in Haro's Barrio de la Estacion. 587+ ha of vineyards mainly Tempranillo; iconic Gran Reserva 890, Gran Reserva 904, Vina Ardanza, Vina Arana.
Brunello estate at 480 m on south-east hills of Montalcino; 53 ha (22 of vineyards). Acquired 2015 by the Tommasi family; traditional long macerations and Slavonian oak aging.
16-acre estate in Yountville's 'Golden Triangle', founded by Paul and Suzie Frank with first vintage 1997; sold 2023 to Jason Rebrook. Cabernet-focused, ~300-400 cases/year.

Saint-Emilion estate of the Malet-Roquefort family since the 17th century; 22 ha on limestone and clay-limestone soils. Premier Grand Cru Classe B until 2022; consultant Stephane Derenoncourt since 2004.
Saint-Julien Second Growth (1855) founded 1725; Merlaut family/Taillon Group since 1997. Single 82-ha undivided vineyard planted 60% Cabernet Sauvignon, 30% Merlot, 7% Cab Franc, 3% Petit Verdot.
Chablis cooperative founded 1923; 300+ growers covering 13 premiers crus and 5 of 7 grands crus. ~25% of all Chablis production; owns Chateau Grenouilles Grand Cru (since 1999).

Tramin estate, fifth-generation family business led by Elena Walch and daughters Julia and Karoline. 60 ha including top vineyards Castel Ringberg (Caldaro) and Kastelaz (Tramin); renowned Gewurztraminer.
Castellina in Chianti estate, 80 ha total with 33 ha of vineyards at avg. 370 m on a south-east-facing amphitheatre. Chianti Classico is 90% Sangiovese, 10% Canaiolo.

Beaune-based natural-wine negociant established 2001 by Philippe Pacalet (nephew of Marcel Lapierre, ex-Prieure-Roch). ~60,000 bottles from grower contracts across Cote de Nuits and Cote de Beaune; no sulfur, native yeasts.
Castelnuovo dell'Abate estate founded 1989, organic-certified; part of ColleMassari group since 2011. 48 ha (20 vineyards) at 190-440 m, 100% Sangiovese with 182 biotypes.
Founded 1974 in Montepulciano; owned since 2008 by Virginie Saverys. ~180 ha fully organic and biodynamic; Italy's largest regenerative wine estate. Flagship Vino Nobile di Montepulciano.

Sancerre family estate (Mellot family since 1513), modern domaine est. 1969. Owns vineyards across all Centre Loire appellations: Sancerre, Pouilly-Fume, Menetou-Salon, Quincy, Reuilly, Coteaux du Giennois, Chateaumeillant.
Family-owned St. Helena estate; the historic Dr. Crane Vineyard was planted 1860 by Dr. George Crane and farmed by the Salvestrin family since 1932. 14.5 acres mainly Cabernet Sauvignon.
One of Napa's oldest family-owned wineries (1895), Solari family since 1948. 115-acre estate vineyard in Calistoga farmed organically; flagship Solari and The Lark Cabernet Sauvignons.
Barolo estate established 1972 by Aldo Vaira in the Vergne hamlet, the highest commune in Barolo (350-480 m). 40 ha organic since the 1970s; traditional aging in Slavonian oak botti.
Fantini Group founded 1994 in Ortona, Abruzzo by Filippo, Valentino and Camillo (the 'three dreamers'); winemaker Alberto Antonini. Fantini brand uses Abruzzo growers; broader group sources widely.

Chateauneuf-du-Pape negociant founded 1931 by Charles Brotte; owns 60+ ha across Chateauneuf, Laudun, Cairanne (Domaine Barville, Chateau de Bord) plus negoce in Gigondas, Vacqueyras, Cote-Rotie, Condrieu.
Saint-Emilion Premier Grand Cru Classe on the limestone plateau; acquired by Etablissements Jean-Pierre Moueix in 2008. 23.5 ha planted 90% Merlot, 10% Cabernet Franc; Chateau Magdelaine merged in.
Beaune negociant founded 1816 by the Jaffelin brothers; cellars in 12th-century Chapitre de Notre Dame. Does not own vineyards, sources from grower contracts across Burgundy.
4th-generation grower in Trepail (Premier Cru, Montagne de Reims). 3 ha across 20 parcels of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, Pinot Meunier on chalky marls; Demeter biodynamic since 2000.
Fourth-generation Gevrey-Chambertin domaine led by Chantal Tortochot. 12 ha across 34 parcels including Le Chambertin, Charmes-Chambertin, Mazis-Chambertin and Clos de Vougeot Grand Crus; organic.
Cru Classe in Martillac, owned by Daniel and Florence Cathiard since 1990. 87 ha single block; produces red and white grand vins; certified organic since 2019 with biodynamic practices.
Margaux Third Growth (1855), owned by the Schyler family since 1926 (Schroder & Schyler negociant). 37 ha across 29 soil types planted to Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot.
Beaune negociant-domaine founded 1750; Bollinger-owned since 1999. 45 ha of estate vineyards (incl. monopole Beaune Clos des Feves), purchases across Cote d'Or, Chablis, Maconnais and Beaujolais.
Muscadet Sevre et Maine estate in Le Landreau founded early 1970s by Guy Bossard, run by Fred Niger today. 17 ha biodynamic-certified for 20+ years; sur-lie Muscadets aged 15-18 months.
Brunello estate in Castelnuovo dell'Abate (south-east Montalcino), founded 1986 by Andrea Cortonesi; first Brunello vintage 1991. 6.5 ha of Sangiovese; flagship Rapace Super Tuscan blend.
Saint-Emilion Grand Cru Classe in Vignobles Silvio Denz portfolio, debut vintage 1998. 7.45 ha 100% Merlot on south-facing clay-limestone hillside; biodynamic since 2020; consultant Michel Rolland.

Pessac-Leognan estate in Leognan, restored from 1996 by Sylvaine Garcin-Cathiard; managed by Helene Garcin. 6.5 ha planted 50% Merlot, 50% Cabernet Sauvignon between Haut-Bailly and Malartic-Lagraviere.
Pauillac Fifth Growth (1855), owned by CA Grands Crus since 2004. 40 ha across three plots planted 63% Cabernet, 37% Merlot.
Family-owned Rutherford winery founded 1980 (vineyards purchased 1964). Focuses on Sauvignon Blanc and Cabernet Sauvignon; Michael Honig manager since 1984; sustainable, solar-powered.

Founded 1967 by Sacchet and Zaccheo families in Dudda (Greve in Chianti). Five estates totaling 500 ha across Chianti Classico, Vino Nobile di Montepulciano and Brunello di Montalcino; ~3M bottles/year.

Maconnais estate in Vinzelles owned by the Bret family since 1947, run by brothers Jean-Philippe and Jean-Guillaume Bret since 2000. ~5 ha; Ecocert organic 2003, Demeter biodynamic 2006; Pouilly-Vinzelles Les Quarts.
Atlas Peak Cabernet project by winemaker Heidi Barrett with John Schwartz and Jim Barbour; ~7-acre vineyard at 2,100 ft on a 45-acre property. Cabernet Sauvignon-led with Petit Verdot.

Family-owned NZ winery established 1998 below Te Mata Peak. Sources from Gimblett Gravels (Hawke's Bay) and Te Muna Road (Martinborough); flagship Sophia Bordeaux blend, Le Sol Syrah, Aroha Pinot Noir.

Saint-Emilion micro-cuvee from a 3.5-ha plot of old vines (60-70 yrs) within Chateau Fombrauge; owned by Bernard Magrez since 1999. 70% Merlot, 30% Cabernet Franc; debut 2000 vintage rated 100 by Parker.

Founded 1883 in Soave by Alberto Bolla, now part of Gruppo Italiano Vini. 300+ ha across Valpolicella Classica, Soave and Lake Garda; credited with releasing the first wine officially labeled 'Amarone' in 1953.

Brunello estate in southern Montalcino, 128 ha (30 ha vineyards) at 350-420 m facing Monte Amiata; replanted from 1998. Owned by Bacci Wines (Marco Bacci); also IGT Toscana with international varieties.

Kenwood (Sonoma Valley) winery founded 1973; vineyard-designate program with Belle Terre, Robert Young and others. Flagship Cinq Cepages Bordeaux blend; Foley Family Wines portfolio with winemaker Lisa Evich.
Montalcino estate purchased 2002 by Riccardo and Jennifer Campinoti; first vintage 2003. ~17 ha across three high-elevation parcels (some above the 600 m DOCG limit); 100% Sangiovese, organic.
Valpolicella Classica estate in San Pietro in Cariano founded 1951 by Angelo Nicolis; ~40-49 ha estate vineyards. Flagship Amarone 'Ambrosan'; ~16,000 cases/year, third generation today.

Pioneer of extreme Sonoma Coast Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, founded 1989 by Walt and Joan Flowers. Camp Meeting Ridge (1989) and Sea View Ridge (1998) vineyards at 1,150-1,875 ft, 2 miles from the Pacific.

Saint-Emilion Grand Cru Classe in Libourne, acquired 2008 by Bernard Arnault and Albert Frere (Cheval Blanc owners); certified organic 2012. 19 ha planted 68% Merlot, 20% Cabernet Sauvignon, 12% Cabernet Franc.
Alexander Valley Vineyards, family-owned in Healdsburg founded 1975 on Cyrus Alexander's 1840s homestead. Known for Cabernet Sauvignon, Sin Zin and the flagship Cyrus Bordeaux blend.
Pomerol estate purchased 1997 by Jean-Hubert Delon (Leoville Las Cases). 32 ha planted 78% Merlot, 21% Cabernet Franc, 1% Cabernet Sauvignon; aged ~30% new oak for 18 months.
Piccini-family Maremma estate near Gavorrano (Gulf of Follonica). 160 ha total, 60 ha organic vineyards; produces Maremma Toscana DOC including Riserva Vesper (Cabernet/Sangiovese) and Sangiovese.
Pessac-Leognan Cru Classe (Graves 1953) in Martillac, owned by the Kressmann family since the late 19th century. 54 ha (45 red, 9 white); reds 55% Cabernet Sauvignon, 40% Merlot, 5% Petit Verdot.

Rutherford-based estate founded 1977 by Mike Grgich and Austin Hills. Five estate vineyards across Napa Valley AVAs (Rutherford, Yountville, Carneros, Calistoga, American Canyon); Regenerative Organic Certified 2022.

Historic Montalcino estate (cellars since 1580), founding member of Brunello consortium 1967. ~125 ha (54 acres Brunello); owned since 2013 by Andre Esteves; Brunello 2018 was Wine Spectator Wine of the Year 2023.
